html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 埋忧续集 - Title for a collection of stories or anecdotes, a continuation of '埋忧集' (Collected Woes).
  • 卷一 - The first volume of the collection.
  • 以都督家居 - Indicates that Liu Ting held the rank of general (都督) but was living at home, possibly retired or on leave.
  • 势张甚 - Indicates that the bandits were very powerful or widespread.
  • 巡抚遣县令郡守请救 - Describes the local officials (prefect and governor) sending a request for help to the provincial governor (巡抚).
  • 少保辞以疾 - Liu Ting refused the request by claiming illness.
  • 复命藩臬往请，坚辞 - The provincial governor sent other officials to persuade him, but he still refused.
  • 皆大怒 - All the officials were very angry.
  • 命医往验，诈则将参之 - They sent a doctor to check if Liu Ting was genuinely ill; if he was faking, they would report him.
  • 奄奄床褥也 - Describes Liu Ting as lying weakly in bed, implying he was indeed ill.
  • 众惶急，策无出 - The officials were anxious and had no solution.
  • 未几，忽报刘将军破贼归矣 - Soon after, they received a report that General Liu had defeated the bandits and returned.
  • 谓：“将军出，吾属固不能知，亦何施此狡狯为？” - They thought, 'We couldn't have known the general would go, so why did he use such cunning?'
  • 贼为陈友谅之裔，蓄谋数传以俟衅 - The bandits were descendants of Chen Youliang, who had been plotting for generations to seize power.
  • 今发不易遏，若知某往，必大备，故密扑之 - If they had known Liu Ting would come, they would have been well-prepared, so he attacked them secretly.
  • 此兵法也 - This is a military strategy.
  • 时方右文 - At the time, there was a strong emphasis on literature and scholarship.
  • 每公会，坐少保诸生下 - During official gatherings, Liu Ting was seated below the scholars.
  • 郡绅士有公宴，醵金不给 - During a public banquet organized by the local gentry, the funds were insufficient.
  • 辄目少保字呼曰：“省吾以办此” - They would call out to Liu Ting by his courtesy name, saying, 'Xingwu, you handle this.'
  • 少保恒什佰于众输办 - Liu Ting always contributed ten or a hundred times more than the others.
  • 或酒酣，令家卒驰马娱宾 - Sometimes, when he was drunk, he would order his servants to ride horses to entertain the guests.
  • 少保兴发，往往上马舞双刀 - When Liu Ting got excited, he would often get on his horse and dance with two swords.
  • 观者但见白气旋绕眩目，不辨其面 - The spectators only saw white mist swirling and blinding their eyes, unable to see his face.
  • 虽奇其艺，亦但作戏玩观也 - Although they were amazed by his skill, they also treated it as a form of entertainment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刘綎 - A famous Ming dynasty general known for his military prowess and loyalty.
  • 陈友谅 - A rebel leader during the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties, known for his role in the founding of the Ming dynasty.
  • 宁州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region during the Ming dynasty.
  • 巡抚 - A high-ranking official responsible for governing a province.
  • 都督 - A military rank equivalent to a general.
  • 藩臬 - Refers to provincial officials, including the governor and the censor.
  • 右文 - A term indicating a strong emphasis on literature and scholarship, often associated with the cultural policies of the Ming dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘綎 - Liu Ting, a Ming dynasty general.
  • 陈友谅 - Chen Youliang, a rebel leader during the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties.
  • 宁州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region during the Ming dynasty.
  • 巡抚 - A high-ranking official responsible for governing a province.
  • 都督 - A military rank equivalent to a general.
  • 藩臬 - Refers to provincial officials, including the governor and the censor.
  • 右文 - A term indicating a strong emphasis on literature and scholarship.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 矫捷有父风 - Agile and having his father's style or manner.
  • 袖箭为绝艺 - Supreme skill with a concealed weapon.
  • 透坚甲，及五六十步 - Penetrate tough armor, up to fifty or sixty steps.
  • 念述止及二十步许，不能穿札 - Only up to about twenty steps, unable to penetrate the armor.
  • 少保有女亦勇 - The Chief Minister has a daughter, who is also brave.
  • 奁具丰盛 - Abundant or plentiful dowry.
  • 突围其家，尽室惶恐 - Break through the defenses, the entire family panic-stricken.
  • 女命婢取软甲披之，率婢挥刀出杀贼 - The daughter ordered the maid to take soft armor and put it on, lead the maids, draw the sword, and go out to kill the bandits.
  • 贼不能支，遁去 - The bandits could not withstand, fled away.
  • 按《明史》列少保平缅、平罗雄、平播酋、平倭、平倮功盖详 - According to the *History of Ming*, the records of his campaigns against the Mien, Luo Xiong, Bo Chiu, Japanese, and Lolo are detailed.
  • 遗平宁州事，以寇一发即灭耳 - Omitting the campaign to pacify Ningzhou, because the bandits were quickly defeated.
  • 至若时俗鄙武，里有达官，缘与少保结婚，至削籍 - As for the contemporary society looking down on martial valor, there were high-ranking officials in the neighborhood, due to marrying the Chief Minister's family, leading to being stripped of their official status.
  • 明之不振有由矣 - The decline of the Ming dynasty, there is a reason for it.
  • 按：少保最善拔距，能纵跃十丈，横跃十丈 - According to, the Chief Minister was best at the ancient sport of *bájù*, could leap ten meters vertically and horizontally.
  • 拔距者，《左传》谓“魏犫距跃三百，曲踊三百”，《汉书》谓“甘延寿少以良家子为羽林，善骑射、投石、拔距，尝超逾羽林亭楼”是也 - The practice of *bájù*, as described in the *Zuo Zhuan* and the *Book of Han*, Gan Yanshou, a young man from a good family, served as a guard in the imperial army, skilled in archery, throwing stones, and *bájù*, once leaped over the guard tower of the imperial army.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少保 - Refers to a high-ranking official, specifically the Chief Minister.
  • 《明史》 - Refers to the official history of the Ming dynasty.
  • 平缅、平罗雄、平播酋、平倭、平倮 - Refers to the campaigns of the Chief Minister against various ethnic groups and foreign invaders during the Ming dynasty.
  • 拔距 - An ancient Chinese sport similar to wrestling, mentioned in both the *Zuo Zhuan* and the *Book of Han*.
  • 羽林 - Refers to the imperial guards in ancient China.
  • 宁州 - Refers to a region in southern China, likely referring to the area around present-day Yunnan Province.
  • 时俗鄙武 - Refers to the contemporary societal attitude that undervalued martial valor.
  • 削籍 - Refers to the act of removing someone from the official register, effectively stripping them of their official status.

Proper Nouns

  • 少保 - The Chief Minister.
  • 《明史》 - The official history of the Ming dynasty.
  • 平缅、平罗雄、平播酋、平倭、平倮 - Campaigns of the Chief Minister against various ethnic groups and foreign invaders.
  • 拔距 - The ancient sport of *bájù*.
  • 羽林 - The imperial guards.
  • 宁州 - A region in southern China.
  • 时俗鄙武 - The contemporary societal attitude toward martial valor.
  • 削籍 - The act of removing someone from the official register.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又按 - Classical phrase meaning 'furthermore' or 'in addition,' often used in footnotes or annotations to provide additional information.
  • 列少保 - Refers to the position of 'Shao Bao' (a high-ranking official) being listed in a specific context.
  • 造次必于礼 - Means 'in haste, one must adhere to propriety,' emphasizing the importance of following etiquette even in hurried situations.
  • 特设榻上枕衾、茵席各一 - Describes the arrangement of bedding on a bed, with '榻' referring to a small bed or couch, '枕衾' to pillows and quilts, and '茵席' to mats or rugs.
  • 键户 - Means 'to lock the door,' indicating a secure closure.
  • 诸公伺焉 - Means 'the other gentlemen waited outside,' suggesting a watchful presence.
  • 无庸 - Means 'it is unnecessary' or 'there is no need,' used here to dismiss the request.
  • 侧身内向 - Means 'turning his body inward,' indicating a change in posture.
  • 漏下四鼓 - Refers to the time of night, specifically 'four drums,' which corresponds to around 4 AM.
  • 诘旦 - Means 'the next day' or 'morning,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 公等为名士 - Refers to 'you all as famous scholars,' addressing a group of respected individuals.
  • 为圣为佛，成忠成孝 - Means 'to become a saint or a Buddha, to achieve loyalty and filial piety,' highlighting moral and spiritual aspirations.
  • 终归黄公 - Means 'ultimately belonging to Mr. Huang,' indicating a conclusion or resolution.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《张瓜田集》 - Refers to a collection of writings by Zhang Guatian, a historical figure.
  • 《明史》 - Refers to the official history of the Ming Dynasty, a significant historical text.
  • 少保 - A high-ranking official title in the Ming Dynasty, equivalent to a minister or counselor.
  • 黄石斋 - Refers to Huang Zhen, a historical figure known for his literary and political contributions.
  • 余中丞 - Refers to a high-ranking official named Yu, likely a governor or provincial governor.
  • 谭友夏 - Refers to Tan Youxia, a historical figure known for his literary and political activities.
  • 金陵 - Refers to Nanjing, a major city in China, historically significant as the capital of several dynasties.
  • 顾氏 - Refers to a courtesan or prostitute named Gu, known for her beauty and intelligence.

Proper Nouns

  • 《张瓜田集》 - The collection of writings by Zhang Guatian.
  • 《明史》 - The official history of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 少保 - A high-ranking official title in the Ming Dynasty.
  • 黄石斋 - Huang Zhen, a historical figure.
  • 余中丞 - A high-ranking official named Yu.
  • 谭友夏 - Tan Youxia, a historical figure.
  • 金陵 - Nanjing, a major city in China.
  • 顾氏 - A courtesan named Gu.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 正而毙 - Die properly.
  • 考终 - Die in accordance with propriety or die a proper death.
  • 日诵 - Recited daily.
  • 貌加丰 - Appearance became more robust or looked healthier.
  • 正命之前夕 - The eve of his execution.
  • 持针线 - Held needles and thread.
  • 事主 - Serve the master.
  • 持酒肉与诀 - Brought wine and meat to bid farewell.
  • 饮啖如平时 - Ate and drank as usual.
  • 酣寝达旦 - Slept soundly until dawn.
  • 盥漱更衣 - Washed and changed clothes.
  • 曩 - Earlier.
  • 以卷索书 - Asked me to write something on a scroll.
  • 言不可旷也 - Cannot let them down.
  • 和墨伸纸 - Mixed ink and spread out the paper.
  • 小楷 - Small regular script.
  • 行书 - Running script.
  • 幅甚长 - The scroll was very long.
  • 以大字竟之 - Finished it in large characters.
  • 加印章 - Stamped his seal.
  • 就刑 - Face execution.
  • 藏 - Kept or stored.
  • 自接公 - After meeting him.
  • 自怼 - Blamed or resented.
  • 归某官 - Returned to a certain official post.
  • 破京师 - Captured Beijing.
  • 谓其夫能死 - Thought that their husband could die.
  • 先就缢 - Hanged themselves first.
  • 不能用 - Could not use them.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明亡 - The fall of the Ming dynasty, referring to the collapse of the Ming dynasty in 1644.
  • 金陵 - Jinling, the former name for Nanjing, the capital of the Ming dynasty.
  • 《尚书》、《周易》 - The Shangshu (Book of Documents) and Zhouyi (Book of Changes), two of the Five Classics of Confucianism.
  • 李自成 - Li Zicheng, the leader of the rebel forces that captured Beijing and ended the Ming dynasty.
  • 京师 - Beijing, the capital of the Ming dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 明 - Ming dynasty.
  • 金陵 - Jinling, the former name for Nanjing.
  • 《尚书》 - Shangshu (Book of Documents).
  • 《周易》 - Zhouyi (Book of Changes).
  • 李自成 - Li Zicheng, the leader of the rebel forces that captured Beijing.
  • 京师 - Beijing, the capital of the Ming dynasty.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A conventional opening phrase used by historians or commentators to introduce their own commentary or interpretation.
  • 以天下为己任 - Literally means 'to take the world as one's responsibility,' referring to a sense of duty to the nation or society.
  • 丁板荡 - A metaphor for times of chaos or turmoil, often referring to periods of political instability.
  • 世际沧桑 - Refers to the passage of time and the changes it brings, often with a connotation of significant transformation or upheaval.
  • 力扶阳九 - A metaphor for trying to support or stabilize a situation in dire straits, often used in the context of restoring order during difficult times.
  • 一死报国 - Refers to the act of sacrificing one's life for the country.
  • 未堪塞责 - Means 'not able to bear the blame' or 'not able to fulfill one's duty satisfactorily.'
  • 从容授命 - Refers to accepting one's fate calmly and gracefully.
  • 不忍言 - Means 'cannot bear to say' or 'cannot bear to speak of.'
  • 身家 - Refers to one's family or personal affairs.
  • 声色货利 - Refers to the temptations of pleasure, wealth, and material gain.
  • 理之不胜夫欲 - Means 'reason cannot overcome desire.'
  • 足令贲、育失其勇，良、平失其智 - Refers to the idea that even the most courageous and intelligent people can be overcome by desire, using historical figures as examples.
  • 惟仙、佛为能制之 - Means 'only immortals and Buddhas can control it.'
  • 圣贤则有动有静 - Refers to the idea that sages and virtuous people can be both active and passive, depending on the situation.
  • 左公罹祸之惨 - Refers to the severe misfortune suffered by Left Gong, likely a historical figure.
  • 凛凛数言 - Describes the few words spoken with a sense of solemnity and respect.
  • 至今犹有生气 - Means 'still have a sense of vitality or life' even after a long time.
  • 平居有如顾氏者 - Refers to someone who is always as refined and cultured as the Gu family, known for their elegance and sophistication.
  • 键户同卧起 - Means 'to lock the door and sleep and wake together,' indicating a close and intimate relationship.
  • 谓能动其一顾哉 - Means 'how could it move him even slightly?'
  • 分定固然 - Means 'fate is naturally determined.'
  • 二公之所同也 - Refers to the shared fate or characteristics of two individuals.
  • 岂二公始念哉 - Means 'was this the original intention of the two individuals?'
  • 可为二公痛哭者矣 - Means 'this is something that can make one weep for the two individuals.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term used by historians or commentators to introduce their own commentary or interpretation.
  • 《望溪文集》 - *The Wangxi Anthology*, a collection of writings by Wang Zhong, a Qing dynasty scholar.
  • 黄公 - Mr. Huang, likely a historical figure whose extraordinary deeds are recorded in the anthology.
  • 左忠毅公 - Left Zhongyi Gong, another historical figure whose story is recorded alongside Mr. Huang's.
  • 余读佛书 - Indicates that the commentator has read Buddhist texts.
  • 迦叶 - Kasyapa, a disciple of the Buddha, known for his wisdom and ascetic practices.
  • 金刚之身 - Refers to the indestructible body of a Buddha or an enlightened being.
  • 《瑜伽论》 - *Yogacara* school of Buddhism, which emphasizes meditation and the cultivation of mental discipline.
  • 魔有四女 - Refers to the four temptresses of Mara, the personification of evil in Buddhism.
  • 菩萨 - Bodhisattva, a being who has achieved enlightenment but chooses to remain in the world to help others.
  • 左公 - Likely a historical figure who suffered a severe misfortune.
  • 顾氏 - Refers to the Gu family, known for their elegance and sophistication.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 对缢 - Refers to the act of hanging oneself, often in a mutual or simultaneous manner.
  • 合欢 - Describes the couple still in their wedding bed, suggesting they had not separated before committing suicide.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《如是我聞》 - Likely a reference to a Buddhist text, but in this context, it is the title of the story.
  • 京师 - The capital city, which could be Beijing or another significant capital depending on the time period.
  • 皋陶 - A legendary figure in Chinese history, known as a wise and just judge, often invoked in legal and moral contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕氏 - The name of the wealthy family.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 萍水之聚 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'though they met briefly like floating萍 (萍水) in water.'
  • 连环易缺，难解同心结 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the linked rings are easily broken, but the heart-knot is hard to untie.'
  • 痴呆佳人才子 - Refers to the foolish but talented couple.
  • 情缘重，怕离别 - Indicates that their emotional bond was strong, and they feared separation.
  • 意切，人路绝 - Describes their deep feelings and the impossibility of their reunion.
  • 共沉烟水阔 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'together they sank into the vast misty waters.'
  • 荡漾香魂何处 - Describes the wandering spirits of the deceased.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花庵《中兴绝妙词选》 - A well-known anthology of Southern Song lyrics, edited by Hua An.
  • 钱塘 - The ancient name for modern-day Hangzhou, a cultural and economic center in Zhejiang province.
  • 西湖 - West Lake, a famous scenic area in Hangzhou, often associated with romantic and tragic stories.
  • 霜天晓角 - A lyric title, possibly referring to a specific form or theme of lyric poetry.
  • 长桥月，短桥月 - Refers to the moon over the Long Bridge and Short Bridge in West Lake, often used in poetry to evoke a sense of time and place.
  • 明崇祯十五年 - The 15th year of the Chongzhen era (1642), a period of political turmoil and transition in Chinese history.
  • 洪承畴 - A prominent Ming general who later defected to the Qing dynasty, a controversial figure in Chinese history.
  • 我朝 - Refers to the Qing dynasty, the ruling power at the time of the text.
  • 江南川湖等省 - Refers to the Jiangnan (south of the Yangtze River), Sichuan, and Hubei provinces, important regions in southern China.

Proper Nouns

  • 花庵 - Hua An, the editor of the *Zhongxing Jueyao Ci Xuan*.
  • 钱塘 - Qiantang, the ancient name for modern-day Hangzhou.
  • 吴礼三 - Wu Li San, a person from Qiantang.
  • 子和 - Zihe, the courtesy name of Wu Li San.
  • 顺受老人词 - The five-volume collection of lyrics by Wu Li San.
  • 陶氏 - Tao Shi, a person (likely a woman) who had a deep emotional bond with Wang.
  • 王生 - Wang, a young man.
  • 霜天晓角 - The title of the lyric composed by Wu Li San.
  • 西湖 - West Lake, a famous scenic area in Hangzhou.
  • 吕氏夫妇 - Lu Shi's couple, a reference to a pair of lovers who died for love.
  • 洪承畴 - Hong Chengchou, a prominent Ming general who later defected to the Qing dynasty.
  • 我朝 - The Qing dynasty, the ruling power at the time of the text.
  • 江南川湖等省 - The Jiangnan, Sichuan, and Hubei provinces.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 按 - Classical particle used to introduce a commentary or annotation.
  • 似犹可任以兵事 - Means 'seems still capable of handling military affairs,' indicating a cautious assessment.
  • 多诬 - Means 'exaggerated' or 'fabricated,' suggesting that the recorded achievements may be unreliable.
  • 两行状 - Refers to two biographies or accounts, likely one from the Ming dynasty and one from the Qing dynasty.
  • 订为一本 - Means 'compiled into one book,' suggesting a combination of the two accounts.
  • 作笑端 - Means 'as a source of amusement,' indicating that the combined accounts were used for entertainment or ridicule.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 承畴 - Refers to Hong Chengchou (洪承畴), a prominent Ming official who later defected to the Qing.
  • 黄梨洲先生 - Refers to Huang Zongxi (黄宗羲), a famous Ming loyalist scholar and historian.
  • 崇祯帝 - Refers to Emperor Chongzhen (崇祯), the last emperor of the Ming dynasty.
  • 十四坛 - Refers to the 14 altars of the Ming imperial sacrifices.
  • 后金 - Refers to the Later Jin, the precursor state to the Qing dynasty, led by Nurhaci and his successors.
  • 金声 - Refers to Jin Sheng, a Ming loyalist who led a rebellion in Huizhou.
  • 江天一 - Refers to Jiang Tianyi, a Ming loyalist who fought alongside Jin Sheng.
  • 御制祭文 - Refers to an imperial edict or eulogy written by the emperor.
  • 右副都御史 - A high-ranking official position in the Ming bureaucracy.
  • 赐祭六坛 - Refers to the imperial sacrifice of six altars, a significant honor.
  • 官为营葬 - Means 'the government arranged for the burial,' indicating official recognition and support.
  • 命建祠都城 - Means 'ordered the construction of a temple in the capital,' another sign of official recognition.
  • 亲临致祭 - Means 'personally attend the sacrifice,' indicating the emperor's personal involvement.
  • 熊襄愍 - Refers to Xiong Xiangmin, a Ming official and loyalist.
  • 轶事 - Refers to anecdotes or lesser-known stories about a person.

Proper Nouns

  • 承畴 - Hong Chengchou
  • 黄梨洲先生 - Huang Zongxi
  • 崇祯帝 - Emperor Chongzhen
  • 后金 - Later Jin
  • 金声 - Jin Sheng
  • 江天一 - Jiang Tianyi
  • 御制祭文 - Imperial edict or eulogy
  • 右副都御史 - High-ranking official position
  • 赐祭六坛 - Imperial sacrifice of six altars
  • 官为营葬 - Government-arranged burial
  • 命建祠都城 - Construction of a temple in the capital
  • 亲临致祭 - Personal attendance at the sacrifice
  • 熊襄愍 - Xiong Xiangmin
  • 轶事 - Anecdotes or lesser-known stories
  • 《明史》 - The History of Ming
  • 崇祯十四年 - 1641 AD
  • 大清兵 - Qing forces
  • 松山 - Songshan
  • 邱民仰 - Qiu Minyang
  • 刍粮 - Grass and grain (food supplies)

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 《全谢山集》 - A collection of works by Qian Qianyi, a prominent scholar and poet of the Ming dynasty.
  • 始宁倪生 - Refers to a person named Ni from Shining (始宁), who is telling the story.
  • 尊人 - Refers to the father of the person being spoken about.
  • 里中仓桥陈氏 - Refers to a specific location and family, likely a place name and surname.
  • 先世《秋曹日录》 - '先世' means ancestors, and '秋曹日录' refers to a record or diary of the Ministry of Justice (秋曹).
  • 熹庙 - Refers to the temple of Emperor Xizong (熹宗) of the Ming dynasty.
  • 狱官 - Refers to a prison official.
  • 魏阉 - Refers to Wei Zhongxian, a notorious eunuch during the late Ming dynasty.
  • 君子 - Refers to virtuous or noble individuals.
  • 东厂 - A secret police organization during the Ming dynasty.
  • 刑部 - The Ministry of Justice in the Ming dynasty.
  • 襄憨 - Refers to Xuanye (襄憨), a historical figure.
  • 藤枕 - A pillow made of rattan.
  • 北辰 - Refers to the North Star, often used symbolically.
  • 西曹郎 - A term for officials in the Ministry of Justice.
  • 赵高语 - Refers to Zhao Gao, a historical figure known for his treacherous actions.
  • 连疏 - Refers to multiple memorials or petitions.
  • 公子兆璧 - Refers to a nobleman named Zhao Bi.
  • 蒲州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region or city.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《全谢山集》 - A significant literary work by Qian Qianyi, known for its historical and literary content.
  • 始宁倪生 - A local figure providing a historical account.
  • 魏阉 - Wei Zhongxian, a powerful eunuch who rose to prominence during the reign of Emperor Xizong, known for his corruption and persecution of officials.
  • 东厂 - A secret police organization established during the Ming dynasty, known for its role in suppressing dissent.
  • 刑部 - The Ministry of Justice, responsible for legal matters and criminal justice.
  • 襄憨 - Xuanye, a historical figure whose actions and fate are described in the text.

  • The sentence '彭法傳在坐，語人曰：“此帶汁（字借作職）諸葛也。”' is clear, but the pun on '汁' and '職' adds a layer of humor or criticism.

  • 承禄取纸笔，大书“岳家父子”四字 - Chenglu took paper and ink and wrote the four characters 'Yue family father and son' in large letters. The phrase '大书' emphasizes the size and prominence of the writing.
  • 人皆悲愤，崇焕亦变色 - Everyone was filled with sorrow and anger. The phrase '悲愤' combines feelings of sadness and indignation. Cao Chunhuan also changed color. The phrase '变色' can mean to become pale or to change one's expression, often indicating shock or anger.
  • 皮岛诸将士共棺殓公，载柩东北去 - The officers and soldiers on Piji Island collectively prepared a coffin for the general. The phrase '共棺殓' indicates a communal effort. Carried the coffin northeast. The direction '东北' (northeast) is significant as it may indicate a return to the general's homeland or a place of honor.
  • 柩至海中不肯行，船反逆而西流 - The coffin would not move when it reached the sea. This phrase suggests a supernatural or symbolic event. The boat flowed backward westward. The phrase '逆而西流' indicates a reversal of direction.
  • 诸将士无如何，乃共拜之，而浮诸海 - The officers and soldiers could do nothing about it. The phrase '无如何' means there was no way to change the situation. They then collectively bowed to it and floated it out to sea. The phrase '浮诸海' means to float it out to sea.
  • 相率东北去，皮岛墟矣 - They all went northeast. The phrase '相率' means to follow one another. Piji Island became desolate. The phrase '墟矣' indicates a state of ruin or abandonment.
  • 失左臂自此始 - The loss of the left arm began here. This phrase suggests a significant event or turning point.
  • 徐泉一复上疏白公冤，不报 - Xu Quanyi again submitted a memorial to clear the general's name, but it was not reported. The phrase '不报' means the memorial was not accepted or acted upon.
  • 泉一遂挂冠归 - Quanyi then resigned and returned home. The phrase '挂冠' is a euphemism for resigning from office.
  • 未几军书旁午 - Soon after, military documents were sent in abundance. The phrase '旁午' means in large quantities.
  • 都城大震 - The capital was greatly alarmed. The phrase '大震' indicates a state of great fear or disturbance.
  • 朝廷知公实枉死 - The court realized that the general had indeed been wrongfully executed. The phrase '实枉死' means truly unjustly killed.
  • 又颇思其功 - And also greatly valued his achievements. The phrase '颇思' means to greatly value or consider.
  • 每肉落一块，人竞买而食之 - Every time a piece of flesh fell, people competed to buy and eat it. The phrase '竞买而食之' indicates a competitive act of consumption.
  • 百刀之誓，至此而符 - The hundred-blade curse was fulfilled here. The phrase '百刀之誓' refers to a curse that the general had made.
  • 时贵人亦得罪 - At the same time, high-ranking officials also incurred guilt. The phrase '得罪' means to incur guilt or blame.
  • 公之为将也严 - The general was strict in his role as a commander. The phrase '为将也严' emphasizes his strictness.
  • 赏罚必信 - He was always trustworthy in rewards and punishments. The phrase '必信' means always trustworthy.
  • 与士卒同甘苦 - He shared the hardships and joys with his soldiers. The phrase '同甘苦' means to share both good and bad times.
  • 有古名将风 - He had the style of an ancient famous general. The phrase '古名将风' refers to the qualities of a renowned general from ancient times.
  • 然恃其功能 - However, he relied on his own abilities. The phrase '恃其功能' means to rely on one's own abilities.
  • 于权要绝不馈遗 - He never gave gifts to powerful officials. The phrase '绝不馈遗' means never to give gifts.
  • 或送白金千两，须人参百斤 - If someone sent him a thousand taels of silver, he would require a hundred catties of ginseng in return. The phrase '须人参百斤' means to require a hundred catties of ginseng.
  • 公但如其价报之 - The general would only return the equivalent value. The phrase '但如其价报之' means to return the equivalent value.
  • 故亦以此致祸 - Therefore, he also caused his own misfortune. The phrase '故亦以此致祸' means therefore, he caused his own misfortune.
  • 铁山、皮岛俱祠公 - Tieshan and Piji Island both built temples to honor the general. The phrase '俱祠公' means both built temples to honor him.
  • 辽左遗民，有挈妻子来，无所归，号泣自经祠下者 - The remaining people of Liaodong, some with their wives and children, came to the temples, having no place to go, and cried out, hanging themselves at the temple gates. The phrase '号泣自经祠下者' means cried out and hanged themselves at the temple gates.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崇焕 - Cao Chunhuan, a prominent military leader during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 公 - Refers to the general, likely referring to Yu Dayou, a famous Ming general.
  • 承禄 - Chenglu, the adopted son of Yu Dayou.
  • 文龙 - Yu Wenlong, Yu Dayou's biological son.
  • 岳家父子 - 'Yue family father and son,' a reference to the Yue family, possibly a metaphor for loyalty and righteousness.
  • 皮岛 - Piji Island, a strategic location in the Bohai Sea.
  • 徐泉一 - Xu Quanyi, a Ming official who submitted a memorial to clear Yu Dayou's name.
  • 铁山 - Tieshan, another strategic location.
  • 辽左 - The eastern part of Liaodong, a region in northeastern China.
  • 百刀之誓 - A curse made by Yu Dayou, possibly referring to a promise or vow.
  • 祠公 - Temples built to honor Yu Dayou.
  • 辽左遗民 - The remaining people of Liaodong, referring to those who survived the turmoil.
  • 自经祠下 - Hanging themselves at the temple gates, a form of protest or despair.
